g.data-narration: Welcome to Programming Raspberry Pi with Python - your gateway to IoT innovation!
g.text: Python to Hardware Flow - IoT Development

g.data-narration: IoT connects everyday devices to the internet, enabling automation. Raspberry Pi serves as the perfect IoT hub for learning and prototyping.
Set up your Raspberry Pi by installing Raspberry Pi OS on a microSD card, connect peripherals, and configure network settings.
Python variables store data - integers for whole numbers, floats for decimals, strings for text, and booleans for true/false values.
Control structures manage program flow - if/else statements make decisions, and for/while loops repeat actions automatically.
Functions are reusable code blocks that perform specific tasks, defined with def keyword and called by name with arguments.
Python modules extend functionality - import time for delays, RPi.GPIO for hardware control, and Adafruit_DHT for sensors.
GPIO pins enable hardware interaction - BCM mode uses chip numbers, BOARD mode uses physical positions. Always cleanup after use.
Control LEDs by setting pin direction to OUTPUT, then use GPIO.HIGH to turn on and GPIO.LOW to turn off the LED.
Read button states by setting pins to INPUT, then use GPIO.input() to read HIGH or LOW based on button press.
The DHT11 sensor measures temperature and humidity. Connect data pin to GPIO17 and use Adafruit_DHT library to read values.
Build a complete temperature monitor: connect DHT11, read sensor data, format output, and display temperature readings in real-time.
Essential Python libraries for Raspberry Pi: RPi.GPIO for low-level control, gpiozero for high-level components, Adafruit libraries for sensors.
Master Raspberry Pi with Python by: setting up the Pi, learning Python basics, controlling GPIO pins, and building IoT projects like temperature monitoring.
